title: Procter & Gamble beats earnings estimates after again raising prices
text: Procter & Gamble Co.'s stock PG, -1.19% rose 1.6% in premarket trade Friday, after the consumer goods giant blew past earnings estimates for its fiscal fourth quarter. The Cincinnati-based parent of brands including Charmin, Crest and Pampers posted net income of $3.384 billion, or $1.37 a share, for the quarter to June 30, up from $3.052 billion, or $1.21 a share, in the year-earlier period.
